weather_report:
Late evening drapes Subiaco in a quilt of broken cloud, the night sky parting now and then to spill a few shy stars. The sun has long since slipped away and with it any bite of UV; the world feels muted and soft, visibility decent but tempered by those passing veils.

As the hours deepen, the clouds ease to scattered, pointing to a cleaner, gentler dawn rather than a harsh glare. It reads as a pleasant stretch overall. Keep it simple: a light layer or an easy shirt you can pocket if the air holds some warmth; sunscreen can wait until after sunrise. These are good, ordinary hours—an unhurried loop around the block, a rinse for the balcony plants, tidying a patient corner of the kitchen, or finishing a chapter by an open window while the cooler air slips in.
